title: Prompt injection vs jailbreak
description: Injection smuggles instructions through data the app trusts; jailbreak coaxes the model past its own guardrails. They need different defenses.
body: Use when reasoning about adversarial input to a model call. Prompt injection hides instructions inside content the application feeds the model (a document, a web page, a tool result) so the model follows attacker text as if it were the system; jailbreak is the user directly trying to bypass the model's safety policy. Do not conflate the two, and avoid assuming provider safety tuning stops injection. Actions: for injection, isolate and label untrusted data, strip instruction-like payloads, and gate privileged tools; for jailbreak, rely on policy plus output checks. Validation signals include an injection test where embedded 'ignore instructions' text fails to change behavior. Risk boundary: injection that can reach a side-effecting tool or exfiltrate data must be denied and escalated.
